exemplar.title:
Replication of the study of Strack and Mussweiler, 1997
The effect of Anchoring Bias on decision-making

detailedExemplarData.title:
Replication of the study of Strack and Mussweiler, 1997
The effect of Anchoring Bias on decision-making

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: Clear statement of investigation aim and its relevance
Accurate description of Dual-Process Model and connection to anchoring bias
Operationalisation of IV and DV in hypotheses
weaknesses: Aim placement is somewhat buried and could be more explicit early on
Practical applications of the studyâs relevance are mentioned but not fully developed
Theory citations are informal rather than following a formal style
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the introduction meets most top-band descriptorsâstating the aim, explaining its relevance, describing theory and operationalised hypothesesâthere is minor vagueness in how the aim is introduced and practical implications could be elaborated. These small gaps warrant a solid band-3/4 score rather than the highest two marks.

strengths: Research design is not only described but also justified (avoiding order effects and communication)
Sampling technique and participant characteristics are explained with rationales
Controlled variables and choice of materials are described and justified
weaknesses: Limitations of the opportunity sampling method (e.g., generalisability) are not discussed
Depth of explanation could be increased by acknowledging potential biases
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands go beyond mere description into explanation, fully meeting the top-band descriptors for exploration. Minor depth issues on sampling limitations do not detract from the overall excellence of this section.

strengths: Appropriate use of MannâWhitney U test with rationale for non-parametric choice
Clear interpretation of statistical findings linked to the hypothesis
Conducted outlier analysis to support data integrity
weaknesses: Typographical error in p-value (âp<0.5â instead of âp<.05â)
Graphs lack full axis labels and units, and bar chart omits error bars
No mention of effect-size measures or tests for normality
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Statistical analysis and interpretation are fundamentally sound, but technical and presentation errors (p-value typo, missing labels and error bars, lack of effect-size discussion) place this in the mid-to-upper band rather than the highest.

strengths: Findings are connected to anchoring theory rather than just restated
Strengths and limitations are clearly stated and linked to the investigation
Modifications are justified and tied explicitly to identified limitations
weaknesses: Discussion of findings is surface-level, lacking integration with wider literature
Broader implications for real-world decision-making are not developed
Some suggested improvements could be more deeply justified
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the student gives relevant strengths, limitations and justified modifications (top-band descriptors), the discussion of findings itself remains at a mid-band levelâlargely descriptive with limited theoretical depthâresulting in a band-2/3 score.

strandSelections.reasoning: A box-and-whisker and bar chart relevant to the hypothesis are provided, but **axes lack full labelling/units and the bar chart omits error bars**, indicating technical inaccuracies.  Thus it meets the âaddresses hypothesis but contains errorsâ descriptor, mid-band score 3/4.

The student **links statistical outcomes to data patterns and to the hypothesis, explicitly rejecting the null**.  Interpretation is clear and accurate, though deeper effect-size comment is absent; 5/6 in the top band is justified.

Both null and research hypotheses are stated; **IV (anchor point: high vs none) and DV (age estimate in years) are explicitly named and operationalised in measurable units**.  Minor wording slips do not obscure clarity, so a score of 5 within the highest band is appropriate.

Opportunity sampling is identified and **rationale is given (availability, non-psychology classes to limit demand characteristics)**.  Participant characteristics are outlined.  These elements fulfil the âexplainedâ descriptor, so 4/4.

Dual-Process Model and anchoring bias are **described with key features (System 1, heuristics) and connected explicitly to the studyâs prediction**.  Although citations are informal and the link could be deeper, the essential theoretical description and relevance to the IV/DV are present and accurate, meriting the top band at 5/6.

Findings are described and **connected to anchoring theory**, but the discussion is surface-level, mainly restating results rather than integrating wider literature or mechanisms in depth.  This fits the âdescribed with referenceâ band; mid-score 3/4.

The student clearly states the aim â to investigate anchoring bias on estimates of Freddie Mercuryâs age at death â and **explains why replicating the classic study in a school context is worthwhile (testing generalisability, everyday decision-making relevance)**.  The explanation is concise but sufficient to move beyond mere identification, warranting the top band, though minor vagueness on practical applications keeps it to the lower end (5/6).

Multiple strengths and limitations (design, standardisation, sampling) are **explained and clearly tied to the studyâs aims and possible confounds**.  Explanations show insight, earning the highest band at 5/6.

Controlled variables (time limit, no devices, seating) and materials are listed **with brief justification for each** (e.g., printed sheets to prevent Googling).  This moves beyond simple description into explanation, fitting the upper band at full 4/4.

Each limitation is followed by a **specific, justified modification** (e.g., matched-pairs to reduce variability, expanding classes for generalisability).  The explicit linkage satisfies the top descriptor; justification is present though not exhaustive, so 5/6.

The student not only describes the independent-measures design but **explains why it was chosen (avoid repetition effects and post-session communication)**.  This explanatory element meets the top band; minor depth issues justify 4/4 rather than perfect.

Means, medians, SDs are calculated and a Mann-Whitney U test is run with justification (non-parametric, <30, independent samples).  Results include U, critical value, p and Z.  **Aside from a typographical âp<0.5â error** the procedure and interpretation are correct, so high-band performance, scored 5/6.

annotations.comment: The background integrates anchoring bias theory with everyday decision-making, setting a clear context for the investigationâs relevance.
This section provides theory on heuristics but lacks an explicit statement of the investigation aim early; the student should relocate or clarify the aim here.
The student gives a precise definition of anchoring bias, supported by citation, which strengthens the theoretical foundation.
Replicating Strack and Mussweilerâs study with Freddie Mercuryâs age is a clear, well-justified aim that connects directly to the generalisability goal.
The independent variable (anchor point condition) is clearly identified, aiding operational clarity.
The dependent variable is operationalised as age estimation in years, providing precise measurement parameters.
The null hypothesis is stated explicitly, but could name IV and DV within it to reinforce variable clarity.
The research hypothesis correctly names IV and DV and operationalises them, meeting high-band standards.
The description of the independent-measures design includes rationale to avoid repetition effects, showing strong research design explanation.
The allocation by class to prevent communication is well justified, improving internal validity.
Opportunity sampling is described with participant demographics, but the limitations of non-random sampling should be acknowledged further.
Selecting non-psychology students to limit demand characteristics is a strong explanatory choice.
Controlled variables (no devices, spread tables, time limit) are described and justified, enhancing experimental control.
Choice of printed materials is well explained to prevent cheating, showing thoughtful materials justification.
Outlier analysis using the interquartile range is an appropriate descriptive step, preventing skewed results.
Table 1 lacks axis labels with units (years) and clear headings for each column, reducing interpretability.
The box-and-whisker plot is unlabelled on axes and missing unit markers; include full labels to clarify data.
Figure 2 (bar chart) omits error bars and axis titles; add error indicators and units to address hypothesis visually.
The rationale for the MannâWhitney U test is generic; include tests for distribution normality and homogeneity of variance.
Typographical error in p-value (âp<0.5â); revise to âp<.05â and ensure significance thresholds are correct.
The findings discussion restates results without deeper linkage to wider anchoring mechanisms; integrate literature to enrich.
The designâs use of independent samples is aptly discussed as a strength and limitation, with clear justification.
Standardisation is recognised well; consider also how individual differences in interpretation of instructions may persist.
Opportunity sample limitation is identified; suggest a stratified sampling approach to enhance generalisability.
The conclusion repeats findings; to strengthen, discuss broader implications for decision-making and real-world contexts.
